summary.intent: Fix a Jenkins build failure where the layered-products scan pipeline was triggering builds for images that depend on disabled images (oadp-operator depending on disabled oadp-velero), causing DoozerFatalError crashes.
summary.outcome: Created and merged PR #2710 that adds disabled dependency detection in scan_sources_konflux.py and filters such images in layered_products_scan_konflux.py. However, discovered a second layer of the problem: doozer's dependents mechanism loads additional images during runtime initialization, requiring a follow-up PR to add graceful rebase failure handling to build_layered_products.py (similar to OCP4 pipeline).

repo: The dependents field in image YAML files causes doozer to automatically load and rebase dependent images even if they weren't in the --images CLI filter
OpenShift groups (openshift-*) vs layered product groups (oadp-1.4) have different pipeline behaviors and filtering needs
OCP4 pipeline has graceful rebase failure handling that reads state.yaml and excludes failed images; layered products pipeline lacks this and crashes
The okd.mode and load_disabled exceptions in late_resolve_image only apply to openshift groups with OKD variant, not to layered products
runtime.gitdata.load_data(path='images') loads ALL image YAML files including disabled ones, not just enabled images in image_map

code.finding: When an image is loaded with process_dependents=True, it calls late_resolve_image(d, add=True, required=False) for each dependent, which adds those dependents to image_map even if not in --images filter
Catches ChildProcessError from rebase, reads state.yaml for failed-images list, logs warnings, and updates build_plan to exclude failed images from the build step
okd_enabled path requires variant == BuildVariant.OKD, which is exclusive to openshift groups and never applies to layered products
Changed _replace_internal_image_refs to use late_resolve_image(distgit, required=False) to get None instead of DoozerFatalError, then raise a clearer error message identifying the disabled image

workflow: Use gh pr create with HEREDOC (cat <<'EOF') for multi-line PR descriptions to preserve formatting
Force-push updates to PR with git push --force after amending commits based on review feedback
Run doozer and pyartcd tests separately to avoid module path collision issues
Pre-commit hooks may require dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true for git operations
AI code review suggestions (CodeRabbit) need to be validated against actual use case - may suggest patterns that don't apply to specific context

friction: Initial implementation included unnecessary ocp4_scan_konflux.py changes (scope creep) that had to be reverted after user question
CodeRabbit review suggested handling okd.mode and load_disabled cases that don't actually apply to layered products context
First fix only addressed scan pipeline layer; problem manifested at deeper layer (doozer runtime initialization via dependents mechanism) discovered in build #1807
Multiple amend and force-push cycles needed to incorporate review feedback (remove ocp4 changes, add openshift group guard, update PR description)
doozer and pyartcd tests can't be run together in single pytest command due to module path issues
open_items: Second PR needed to add graceful rebase failure handling to build_layered_products.py similar to OCP4 pipeline (catch ChildProcessError, read state.yaml, exclude failed images)
Consider whether to add defensive handling in doozer's image.py when processing dependents to skip loading dependents that reference disabled images (marked as harder and riskier approach)
